remember: Students will be able to recall and write the major features of atomic nucleus.
Students will be able to identify and describe the different parts of the nucleus.
Students will be able to list the major characteristics of nuclear forces.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of radioactivity, its types, and the process of radioactive decay.
Students will be able to describe the nuclear reaction and types of nuclear reactions.
Students will be able to understand the concept of binding energy per nucleon and its significance.
apply: Students will be able to calculate and apply the concepts of mass defect, binding energy, and nuclear force.
Students will be able to solve problems related to nuclear reactions, such as alpha, beta, and gamma decay.
analyze: Students will be able to compare and contrast the different types of nuclear reactions and their applications.
Students will be able to analyze the factors that affect nuclear stability and the role of binding energy.

ncert_objectives: Explain the formation of a nucleus from protons and neutrons.
Describe the different types of radioactive decay.
Explain the concept of binding energy and its significance in nuclear stability.
board_exam_objectives: The types of nuclear reactions (radioactive decay, nuclear fission, and nuclear fusion).
The concept of binding energy, mass defect, and nuclear stability.
The applications of nuclear reactions and the importance of nuclear physics in everyday life.
